title: 'Impact evolution' — Oct 2018 title-only seed
html:
TL;DR

  • Title-only Apple Note from Oct 9, 2018, 9:06am — Yahir is 16, sophomore-junior transition at Grants Pass High. Title is the entire artifact: "Impact evolution." Body is empty. 7-second create→modify gap.
  • One of the earliest-dated Apple Notes in the corpus (apple_notes_pk: 43) — Yahir is 16 and already opening notes named Impact evolution and closing them empty. The phrase reads as a thesis-stub for a frame he was already working out — how does the way I create impact have to evolve over time.
  • Pure : title-as-essay-prompt, body never filled. The same form as the Mar-2019 "How the mind works" note and the Sept-2020 "transmute" note.
  • The phrase "impact evolution" anticipates by years the explicit framing in excessively net positive person (2023) and debt of gratitude — the disposition that the form of giving back has to keep upgrading as one's capacity grows.
  • Sits at the head of the mentors-from-afar timeline — the era when Yahir is consuming John Maxwell-flavored leadership content and spinning the abstractions back out as one-line manifesto-titles.

Source

"Impact evolution"

Why it matters in the graph

  • Earliest titled-aphorism in the corpus — the prototype for an entire seven-year habit.
  • Anchors the teen-era impact-thinking cluster that later densifies into the Al Forthan speech / Ford application / scholarship-essay arc of 2021.
  • Companion to the 2018-era fear of not doing disposition: the title is had, the writing is not done — the form itself is the disposition.

Open questions

  • Was this prompted by a specific October-2018 read or talk? No surrounding context.

Wikilinks
